## Initial Results {.nav-proposed}
We apply these oracles to a simulated PBL loop under a 2x2 study.

<div style="display: flex; gap: 1em;">
<div style="flex: 10;">
<div style="margin-top: -1em;">
![](images/cumulative_regret.svg){width="100%"}
</div>

<div style="margin-top: -2em;">
![](images/l2_distance.svg){width="100%"}
</div>
</div>
<div style="flex: 9;">
We apply Wilcoxon signed-rank test to our results:

1. We find that our oracle predicts better preferences after an exploration stage $(p < 0.0001)$.
2. There is an inherent cost to an exploration stage for stationary agents in the form of additional regret $(p < 0.00001)$.
</div>
</div>
